reasoning: The yfinance tool generated a combined statistical estimate of 0.56 for Nike's stock to exceed the Dec 7 threshold by Dec 14. This is supported by recent upward momentum over the last two weeks, where the stock climbed from ~$60 to ~$65.43, partly in response to recent leadership changes by the CEO in early December. For the first resolution date (Dec 14), which falls right before the Q2 earnings release on Dec 18, I maintain a slight bullish bias derived from the statistical anchor, setting it at 0.54. Because earnings reactions are highly unpredictable and the turnaround is still considered a work in progress, variance will increase significantly after Dec 18. Following the instruction to compress further-out dates toward 0.5 due to increasing uncertainty over forecast horizons, I assign 0.52 to January 6 and 0.51 to March 7.
